summary: Iteration 57 — bookmarks importer flow tested end-to-end on https://mindmap-studio-5.preview.emergentagent.com/app?unlock=mind-mapper67. All 6 FIX scenarios + REGRESSION + mobile sanity pass. No backend testing done (frontend-only request).

issues: Recurring console error: 'Maximum update depth exceeded. This can happen when a component calls setState inside useEffect…'. Fires repeatedly on /app, also during/after the bookmarks import flow. Not introduced by the importer (importer code itself has no useEffect), but worth investigating since it spams the console and can degrade perf. Likely from an existing Studio effect with unstable deps.

fix_results.FIX1_button_visible_and_modal: PASS — [data-testid=btn-import-bookmarks] present below New/Open. Click opens [data-testid=bookmarks-import-modal] with header 'Import browser bookmarks'.
fix_results.FIX2_modal_ui: PASS — 'How to export' section with Chrome/Firefox/Safari instructions visible. Dropzone visible. Hidden file input with accept='.html,.htm,text/html' present.
fix_results.FIX3_reject_non_html: PASS — Uploading /tmp/not_bookmarks.txt triggers toast: "That doesn't look like a bookmarks .html file".
fix_results.FIX4_sanity_check_no_DL: PASS — Uploading random .html (no <DL>) triggers toast: "File doesn't look like a Netscape bookmarks export. Re-export from your browser."; preview NOT shown.
fix_results.FIX5_happy_path: PASS — Uploading minimal Netscape bookmarks file shows preview with linkCount=3 (Bookmarks: 3) and folderCount=1 (Folders: 1). Title input editable, defaulted to filename 'test_bookmarks'. Edited to 'My Test Bookmarks Map'. Confirm fires toast 'Imported 3 bookmarks across 1 folder', modal closes, new map becomes active in Studio canvas (root node 'My Test Bookmarks Map' visible with Research/Alpha/Beta/Gamma children). localStorage.mindmapper.maps.v1 contains the new map with source='bookmarks-import' and matching title.
fix_results.FIX6_cancel_and_close: PASS — After upload+preview, [data-testid=bookmarks-import-cancel] returns to dropzone (preview gone). [data-testid=bookmarks-import-close] (X) fully dismisses the modal.
fix_results.REGRESSION: PASS — [data-testid=mm-tb-connect-mode] still present in toolbar. [data-testid=btn-open-output] still present in sidebar. Studio mounts and renders cleanly.
fix_results.mobile_390px: PASS — Import button visible and clickable at 390x844; modal opens and is fully readable on mobile width.

action_items: (Optional, not blocking) Investigate the recurring 'Maximum update depth exceeded' React warning on /app — pre-existing, but persistent in console.

(Optional polish) Default title strips '.html' but on a real Chrome export the file is literally 'bookmarks_4_28_26' so the default already looks reasonable; consider trimming common prefixes like 'bookmarks_' for nicer defaults.
critical_code_review_comments: BookmarksImportModal.jsx: clean, self-contained, uses data-testid throughout. Good.
bookmarksImport.js: handles both Chrome (sibling DL) and Firefox (nested DL) shapes via :scope queries — verified functionally with the synthesised file.
Importer correctly tags map.source='bookmarks-import' and persists to mindmapper.maps.v1 — confirmed in localStorage.
Hidden file input uses `hidden` attr; tests must use state='attached' or set_input_files directly (not blocking — works in production via the visible label).
seed_data_creation: Created 3 fixture files: /tmp/test_bookmarks.html (valid Netscape, 3 links / 1 folder), /tmp/not_bookmarks.txt, /tmp/random.html
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bookmarks importer is fully functional end-to-end: button, modal, validation (file type + DL sanity), preview, editable title, commit, save to localStorage with source='bookmarks-import', toast, modal close, cancel/close-X paths, plus Chrome+Firefox shape parsing. Pre-existing 'Maximum update depth exceeded' console warning on /app is unrelated and was already present in iter 48-56.
